text: The river passes the city's eastern wall through a pair of iron-grated culverts. Above the culverts, a stone quay stretches forty paces along the bank, and this is where people come to fish, wash things they do not want washed at the baths, or simply sit and watch the water move. The river knows nothing of the city's troubles and does not care to learn.
The water is dark and cold-looking, moving with quiet purpose toward somewhere else. A heron stands motionless on a rock mid-current, waiting with more patience than you possess.
